gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5/high review found one P1 fail-open case: internal GitHub visibility could pass because readiness only checked private=true. The helper now also requires visibility == 'public', the regression test covers internal visibility, focused tests pass, and the re-review approved with no remaining merge blockers.
scope: public document package module and script metadata
behavioral tests for fail-closed readiness, token redaction, and CLI JSON output
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_github_governance.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q (46 passed)

secret scan: rg -n "dapi[0-9A-Za-z]{20,}|sk-proj|gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9_]{20,}|lsv2_|-----BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----" README.md CONTRIBUTING.md pyproject.toml src tests databricks pr-evidence .github; only existing fake LangSmith detector fixtures matched
GPT-5.5/high review found a P1 visibility fail-open issue; fixed with a visibility == public check and regression test; re-review approved
what_changed: Added a stdlib-only GitHub governance helper and CLI that reads repository metadata plus main branch protection from the GitHub API.
Made release-readiness fail closed unless the repository is public and main branch protection includes the required CI and PR-review controls.
Wired the helper into the public document package submodule map, console scripts, package docs, and tests.
why: Release readiness needs a machine-checkable sidecar proving whether GitHub visibility and branch protection are actually enforceable, without mutating repository settings or persisting credentials.
